nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda was born in 1280, in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. She married William Thirning in 1309, in Little Harrowden, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1321, in her hometown, at the age of 41.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda(은)는 1280년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1309년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 리틀 해로우든에서 William Thirning(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1321년 그녀의 고향에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Matilda родилась в 1280 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Thirning в 1309 г. в местности литл харроуден, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1321 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 41 год.
